name: Dzung Tri
role: CTO · Technical Founder · Applied AI Builder
title: Engineering leadership for applied AI, product architecture, and AI-native software execution
description: I work where architecture, delivery, and product value meet. My path runs from product engineering in Japan, through co-founding and scaling Fabbi, to applied AI systems and recent work on LLM workflows, coding agents, and legacy modernization.
footerNote: Personal profile and writing on practical technology execution.
contactCta: Open to CTO, engineering leadership, applied AI, and AI-native software conversations.
heroNote: Delivery-first technology leadership shaped by product engineering in Japan, cross-border execution in Vietnam and Japan, and applied AI work built for production rather than demos.

heroStats.label: Experience
heroStats.value: 12+ years in engineering and product delivery

heroStats.label: Scale
heroStats.value: Fabbi to 250+ engineers and ~$4M annual revenue by 2021

heroStats.label: Current
heroStats.value: LLM systems, coding agents, reverse engineering workflows

currentSignals: Architecture decisions anchored in delivery reality, not technology theater.
Applied AI used as part of engineering systems and operating workflows.
Hands-on depth across product, platform, AI, and cross-functional execution.

operatingPrinciples.title: Delivery-first architecture
operatingPrinciples.body: I favor technical decisions that survive real constraints: deadlines, team maturity, operations, scale, and cost.

operatingPrinciples.title: Applied AI under production pressure
operatingPrinciples.body: My AI work is grounded in deployable systems, from OCR and facial recognition to coding agents and software engineering workflows.

operatingPrinciples.title: Technical leadership with implementation depth
operatingPrinciples.body: I work comfortably from system design and code-level reasoning up to team structure, process, and product direction.

trajectory.title: Japan product engineering
trajectory.body: Worked at Kayac and DeNA in Tokyo on game systems, realtime networking, AI in games, and search optimization for large production platforms.

trajectory.title: Community product building
trajectory.body: Co-created Mazii as a long-running product for Japanese learners in the Vietnam-Japan ecosystem, combining language tooling and sustained product iteration.

trajectory.title: Fabbi founding and scale-up
trajectory.body: Co-founded Fabbi, built engineering standards and delivery capability, and helped scale the organization into a major Vietnam-Japan software engineering team.

trajectory.title: Applied AI products
trajectory.body: Led Fabbi AI and Fvision across computer vision, eKYC, OCR, smart building, embedded constraints, and edge-plus-cloud deployment concerns.

trajectory.title: Generative AI and AI-native workflows
trajectory.body: Focused on open-source LLMs, tokenizer fundamentals, LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Google AI SDK, coding agents, and legacy-to-document modernization pipelines.

signatureWork.name: Fabbi
signatureWork.summary: Built the engineering foundation of Fabbi from the founding stage, set delivery standards, and led cross-border execution for clients in Vietnam and Japan.
highlights: Helped scale the engineering organization to 250+ engineers by 2021.
Worked on multi-million-dollar software programs for large clients across markets.
Established the technical base for Fabbi AI across OCR, eKYC, chatbot, and computer vision workstreams.

signatureWork.name: TV Tokyo / txbiz
signatureWork.summary: Joined from the early phase of a live streaming platform, video management system, and website-plus-app ecosystem for TV Tokyo, with ownership spanning architecture, team leadership, and Agile delivery rhythm.
highlights: From-scratch platform build for a major Japanese media brand.
Designed microservice architecture for a video-centric product landscape.
Worked across web platform, app ecosystem, streaming workflow, and operational delivery concerns.

signatureWork.name: Fvision
signatureWork.summary: Led the Fvision program for facial recognition and smart building access management, combining deep learning, Android customization, sensors, embedded hardware, and deployment engineering.
highlights: Built as a deployable product with AI, IoT, and hardware integration in one system.
Connected model work to operations, physical environments, and enterprise use cases.

signatureWork.name: Mazii
signatureWork.summary: Built a Japanese learning and lookup product for learners in the Vietnam-Japan ecosystem, reflecting long-term interest in language tooling and useful community products.
highlights: Started as an independent side project with community utility at the center.
Grew into a recognizable product in the Japanese learning space.
Represents an enduring product mindset beyond client delivery work.

focusAreas.name: AI for software delivery
focusAreas.description: Applying Generative AI to coding, review, documentation, and architecture understanding for real engineering teams.
focusAreas.stack: Open-source LLMs, coding agents, workflow orchestration

focusAreas.name: Legacy reverse engineering
focusAreas.description: Converting legacy source systems into structured documentation and reusable technical knowledge to accelerate modernization.
focusAreas.stack: Source analysis, agent workflows, documentation pipelines

focusAreas.name: Agentic product development
focusAreas.description: Building products and internal systems that use AI agents as part of the operating model rather than as isolated experiments.
focusAreas.stack: LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Google AI SDK

focusAreas.name: Model-level understanding
focusAreas.description: Studying model mechanics deeply enough to make grounded implementation decisions instead of trend-driven choices.
focusAreas.stack: Tokenizer basics, GAN foundations, inference and integration tradeoffs

recognition.title: Top 20 young technology leaders
recognition.detail: Featured by VnExpress in the 2021 Top 20 young technology leaders selection.

recognition.title: Sao Khue 2021
recognition.detail: Fvision was recognized with Sao Khue 2021.

recognition.title: Cross-border delivery credibility
recognition.detail: Worked across Vietnam and Japan with clients including TV Tokyo and Sony Music Japan.

writingTracks: Applied AI architecture decisions and delivery tradeoffs
CTO operating systems: team design, quality, and execution rhythm
AI-native software engineering with coding agents
Case notes from shipped systems, experiments, and modernization work
